# KBC Financial Operations Update

*Building a clearer, more accountable, and more sustainable system*

> We are not trying to replace one person with another person. We are trying to build a financial system that is clear, accountable, transferable, and sustainable.

| Why This Matters | What We Are Trying to Fix |
| --- | --- |
| The Treasurer transition gives KBC an opportunity to strengthen the system.<br>Clear financial practices help protect trust and support good stewardship.<br>Better documentation makes the work easier to review and hand off. | The Treasurer role has become broad and difficult for one person to carry.<br>Routine bookkeeping and officer-level review need clearer separation.<br>Some financial processes need clearer written steps and committee review. |

| Proposed Direction | How Work Will Be Divided |
| --- | --- |
| The Treasurer remains an accountable elected officer.<br>Bookkeeping or financial administration support may be considered if approved.<br>The goal is a healthier structure, not a rushed staffing decision. | Treasurer: accountability, review, reporting, and Finance Committee coordination.<br>Bookkeeper / Financial Administrator, if approved: records, reimbursements, reports, deposits, and documentation.<br>Finance Committee: controls, software, budget review, reimbursements, and monthly reporting.<br>Personnel Committee: role description, application, hiring, compensation, and evaluation questions. |

| What Comes Next | What the Church Is Being Asked to Support |
| --- | --- |
| Leadership will review the proposed structure.<br>Finance Committee and Personnel Committee will review the parts assigned to them.<br>Final recommendations that require church approval will come back to the church. | A careful review process.<br>Clearer responsibilities and better documentation.<br>Good stewardship, accountability, and sustainability.<br>Future church votes where bylaws, budget, policy, or church practice require them. |
